coverage.lo: Define what a token is and explain how text is split into tokens using subword tokenisation.
Describe the next-token prediction objective and explain how a model learns by adjusting its parameters on large amounts of text.
Explain how an LLM generates text one token at a time during inference, distinguishing the prefill phase from the decode phase.
Describe the role of the attention mechanism in allowing the model to relate tokens across the context window.
Identify practical implications of tokenisation and context windows when interacting with an LLM.

beginner_fairness_and_scope.notes: All stems use plain language. Acronym LLM defined in corpus §4 (Introduction); backpropagation appears only in answer options, not stems. All questions test concepts within topic 3.4 corpus §5.1–§7.1. Distractors are plausible beginner misconceptions (dictionary lookup, memorisation, database retrieval, parallelism confusion, knowledge-cutoff conflation) requiring no untaught knowledge to evaluate.

verdict: Pass — all five checks clean. Length bias rate dropped from 0.60 (attempt 2) to 0.20 (attempt 3); only q1 still flags (correct 111 vs threshold 110.0, 1-char margin) but rate 0.20 is well below the 0.40 cutoff. Positional bias, distractor plausibility, objective coverage, and beginner-fairness all pass.
